If the armor absorbs an attack it slows the overall start-up by 1F (27F total instead of 26F) and incurs 6F hitstop for each projectile hit absorbed (making it less likely to punish the opponent after absorbing projectiles)
Absorbing an attack causes the player to take 1/2 the damage in recoverable health (but will KO if this would bring the player's health below zero)
Stun/Dizzy occurs when a Wall Splat connects on an opponent in Burnout

Stun gives a free combo follow-up for the next 195F after hitting the wall, followed by a HKD if the player falls to the ground untouched (the last 7F before hitting the ground has a crouch-sized hurtbox but is considered standing, allowing for unique combo setups)

Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo (applies to wall splat on HIT, crumple, Burnout dizzy on hit/block, or any character-specific means of comboing after a regular Drive Impact)

Incurs a 20% multiplier damage scaling penalty after a Wall Splat on BLOCK - Because the blocked Drive Impact is not considered a "hit" for the combo, the next attack can incur its own separate damage scaling as a combo starter

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Causes a 4F screen freeze after the 6th start-up frame, which allows the opponent to perform a simple OS (inputting their buttons during the buffer window) to prevent their next attack from coming out
Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)
Framedata shown in parentheses refers to the Drive Rush version used from a Parry

Drive Rush recovery is only 24F total (23F if done from a Parry) - but the enhanced dash itself is 46F total (45F if done from Parry) and you can cancel it as early as frame 9 (frame 11 if done from a Parry)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Applies an extra damage scaling penalty after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (no damage scaling if you use Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Costs 3 bars of Drive Gauge if canceled into from a normal attack
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge if canceled into from Parry (for a total of 1 bar of Drive Gauge when you factor in the initial 1/2 bar cost of the Parry)

Normals used out of Drive Rush carry additional forward momentum (but throws and specials immediately halt all momentum, which can be a useful strategy to punish opponents for trying to interrupt your Drive Rush)
Parry Drive Rush can only be initiated after frame 3 of the Parry (but can be performed immediately after recovering from a successful parry)

Parry Drive Rush cannot be initiated with less than 1/2 of a Drive bar (as the initial Parry cost will put the user into Burnout) - But whiff canceling a Light normal into Drive Rush can achieve a similar effect
Cannot cancel Drive Rush into a Parry (inputting MP+MK results in a MK normal coming out instead)

Absorbing an attack causes the player to take 1/2 the damage in recoverable health (but will KO if this would bring the player's health below zero)
Stun/Dizzy occurs when a Wall Splat connects on an opponent in Burnout

Stun gives a free combo follow-up for the next 195F after hitting the wall, followed by a HKD if the player falls to the ground untouched (the last 7F before hitting the ground has a crouch-sized hurtbox but is considered standing, allowing for unique combo setups)

Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo (applies to wall splat on HIT, crumple, Burnout dizzy on hit/block, or any character-specific means of comboing after a regular Drive Impact)

Incurs a 20% multiplier damage scaling penalty after a Wall Splat on BLOCK - Because the blocked Drive Impact is not considered a "hit" for the combo, the next attack can incur its own separate damage scaling as a combo starter

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Causes a 4F screen freeze after the 6th start-up frame, which allows the opponent to perform a simple OS (inputting their buttons during the buffer window) to prevent their next attack from coming out
Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)

Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge

Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring

Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)

Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)

Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)

Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)

Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)
Framedata shown in parentheses refers to the Drive Rush version used from a Parry

Drive Rush recovery is only 24F total (23F if done from a Parry) - but the enhanced dash itself is 46F total (45F if done from Parry) and you can cancel it as early as frame 9 (frame 11 if done from a Parry)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Applies an extra damage scaling penalty after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (no damage scaling if you use Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Costs 3 bars of Drive Gauge if canceled into from a normal attack

Absorbing an attack causes the player to take 1/2 the damage in recoverable health (but will KO if this would bring the player's health below zero)
Stun/Dizzy occurs when a Wall Splat connects on an opponent in Burnout

Stun gives a free combo follow-up for the next 195F after hitting the wall, followed by a HKD if the player falls to the ground untouched (the last 7F before hitting the ground has a crouch-sized hurtbox but is considered standing, allowing for unique combo setups)

Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo (applies to wall splat on HIT, crumple, Burnout dizzy on hit/block, or any character-specific means of comboing after a regular Drive Impact)

Incurs a 20% multiplier damage scaling penalty after a Wall Splat on BLOCK - Because the blocked Drive Impact is not considered a "hit" for the combo, the next attack can incur its own separate damage scaling as a combo starter

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Causes a 4F screen freeze after the 6th start-up frame, which allows the opponent to perform a simple OS (inputting their buttons during the buffer window) to prevent their next attack from coming out
Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)
Framedata shown in parentheses refers to the Drive Rush version used from a Parry

Drive Rush recovery is only 24F total (23F if done from a Parry) - but the enhanced dash itself is 46F total (45F if done from Parry) and you can cancel it as early as frame 9 (frame 11 if done from a Parry)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Applies an extra damage scaling penalty after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (no damage scaling if you use Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Costs 3 bars of Drive Gauge if canceled into from a normal attack
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge if canceled into from Parry (for a total of 1 bar of Drive Gauge when you factor in the initial 1/2 bar cost of the Parry)

Normals used out of Drive Rush carry additional forward momentum (but throws and specials immediately halt all momentum, which can be a useful strategy to punish opponents for trying to interrupt your Drive Rush)
Parry Drive Rush can only be initiated after frame 3 of the Parry (but can be performed immediately after recovering from a successful parry)

Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)

Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

If the armor absorbs an attack it slows the overall start-up by 1F (27F total instead of 26F) and incurs 6F hitstop for each projectile hit absorbed (making it less likely to punish the opponent after absorbing projectiles)

Stun/Dizzy occurs when a Wall Splat connects on an opponent in Burnout

Stun gives a free combo follow-up for the next 195F after hitting the wall, followed by a HKD if the player falls to the ground untouched (the last 7F before hitting the ground has a crouch-sized hurtbox but is considered standing, allowing for unique combo setups)

Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo (applies to wall splat on HIT, crumple, Burnout dizzy on hit/block, or any character-specific means of comboing after a regular Drive Impact)

Incurs a 20% multiplier damage scaling penalty after a Wall Splat on BLOCK - Because the blocked Drive Impact is not considered a "hit" for the combo, the next attack can incur its own separate damage scaling as a combo starter

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring

Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)

12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)

Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)

Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry

Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact

Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)

Regular KDA without Wall Splat +35 KD | Wall Splat on Hit +65 KD | Wall Splat on Block +72 HKD
KDA can vary greatly depending on how high the opponent wall splats (higher KDA the higher they wall splat)
Advantage after Wall Splat can vary depending on distance from the corner (farther from corner gives higher advantage)

Crumples the opponent on Punish Counter or after absorbing at least 1 attack during start-up (+21 frames before the crumple state goes airborne, followed by airborne state on frames 22-46 before they are grounded)
Puts airborne opponents into a long spinning free juggle state when hit as a Punish Counter or after absorbing an attack

If the armor absorbs an attack it slows the overall start-up by 1F (27F total instead of 26F) and incurs 6F hitstop for each projectile hit absorbed (making it less likely to punish the opponent after absorbing projectiles)
Absorbing an attack causes the player to take 1/2 the damage in recoverable health (but will KO if this would bring the player's health below zero)
Stun/Dizzy occurs when a Wall Splat connects on an opponent in Burnout

Stun gives a free combo follow-up for the next 195F after hitting the wall, followed by a HKD if the player falls to the ground untouched (the last 7F before hitting the ground has a crouch-sized hurtbox but is considered standing, allowing for unique combo setups)

Incurs 20% damage scaling penalty if used as the first hit of a combo (applies to wall splat on HIT, crumple, Burnout dizzy on hit/block, or any character-specific means of comboing after a regular Drive Impact)

Incurs a 20% multiplier damage scaling penalty after a Wall Splat on BLOCK - Because the blocked Drive Impact is not considered a "hit" for the combo, the next attack can incur its own separate damage scaling as a combo starter

Wall Splat puts opponent into a special juggle state where the next move freely connects and continues the juggle even if it normally wouldn't (similar to a Forced Knockdown or Drive Rush juggle properties)
Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Causes a 4F screen freeze after the 6th start-up frame, which allows the opponent to perform a simple OS (inputting their buttons during the buffer window) to prevent their next attack from coming out
Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)
Framedata shown in parentheses refers to the Drive Rush version used from a Parry

Drive Rush recovery is only 24F total (23F if done from a Parry) - but the enhanced dash itself is 46F total (45F if done from Parry) and you can cancel it as early as frame 9 (frame 11 if done from a Parry)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Applies an extra damage scaling penalty after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (no damage scaling if you use Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Costs 3 bars of Drive Gauge if canceled into from a normal attack
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge if canceled into from Parry (for a total of 1 bar of Drive Gauge when you factor in the initial 1/2 bar cost of the Parry)

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring

Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)

12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)

Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)

Does not cause Wall Splat if the Drive Impact connects as a combo or a true blockstring with no gaps (LOCK icon appears on-screen)

It is possible to "combo" into Stun if the opponent has under 1.5 Drive bars - a blocked DI Wall Splat from slightly outside the corner into a second DI on hit will cause Burnout and Stun (because the blocked DI is not technically considered a combo starter)
Costs 1 bar of Drive Gauge
Cinematic slowdown occurs if both players activate Drive Impact within range of each other (granting a Punish Counter to the player who activates it later)
Attempting to counter with DI vs. DI with under 400 HP will result in a KO, as the second player will not have enough health to absorb it

Clashes with other Drive Impacts and refunds the Drive gauge cost if they collide on the same active frame (start at exactly the same time, or 1F apart if the first Drive Impact absorbs a strike) - The meter refund does not prevent Burnout from occurring
The scrub killer
Has two versions: one performed during blockstun or parry stun (20F start-up) and one performed as a wake-up reversal (18F start-up)
Fully invincible on frames 1-22 (blockstun) / frames 1-20 (reversal)
Armor Break (blows through all armor like Drive Impact, but not counters like JP's Amnesia)
Trying to Drive Reversal after the blockstun ends results in a Drive Impact
Drive Reversal immediately removes the current blockstun you're in and starts the attack

Causes a 4F screen freeze after the 6th start-up frame, which allows the opponent to perform a simple OS (inputting their buttons during the buffer window) to prevent their next attack from coming out
Safe on block (-2) when used against an opponent in Burnout
Costs 2 bars of Drive Gauge
If the opponent tries to throw against a wake-up Drive Reversal, it causes a 12F screen freeze and refunds 1 bar of Drive gauge (but does not prevent Burnout)
Guaranteed to interrupt any Drive Rush canceled normal if performed immediately out of blockstun (unless the opponent reacts with an invincible move or counter)
Parries all strikes and projectiles (high and low) from frame 1
12F parry window (can hold the parry to extend the parry window)
Perfect Parry occurs on the first 2 active frames (cannot achieve Perfect Parry if you buffer into parry by holding it during Reversal windows; Perfect Parries have to be timed properly)
Can still hold charge directions during Parry (allowing characters with charge specials to stand block before performing their reversal)
Can block during the recovery frames of a regular parry (can still get hit during the recovery frames if you don't block correctly)
Getting thrown or hit at any point during the recovery frames of a whiffed parry attempt results in a Punish Counter
Successfully parrying a move puts you into the same amount of blockstun as that move would regularly do on block (but a Perfect Parry has only 1F of recovery)
Throw invincible during the recovery of a successful parry (just like during blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a strike has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack (basically a guaranteed punish opportunity except for safe jump setups)
Perfect Parrying a strike requires holding the correct block direction (Down+Back for low strikes, Back for high/mid strikes)
Attempting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" that builds an extra 3000 Drive Gauge and plays a distinct audio/visual cue

After a successful Perfect Parry, you suffer a 50% penalty to your Damage, Drive Gauge Generation, and Drive Gauge Damage on any of your following attacks when punishing the opponent (in addition, both you and the opponent suffer a 20% penalty to your Super Meter gain during this period)
Fully invincible for 6F after a successful Perfect Parry against a strike, which carries over to any attack you perform (ensuring a 5F or faster punish will always interrupt multi-hit attacks)
Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 10 frames of recovery and applies the same 50% scaling penalties (and 20% Super Meter penalty) on your following attacks when punishing the opponent
Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will result in a regular parry for the strike attack (effectively ignoring the projectile's hitstop / blockstun)
Perfect Parrying a Strike and Projectile on the same frame will give a screen freeze like you Perfect Parried a Strike, but still give you 10F recovery as if you had Perfect Parried a Projectile
Can cancel into Drive Rush during active frames 3 and onward (fastest Drive Rush method is to buffer a forward input before doing parry + forward again)
Can cancel into Drive Reversal from a successful parry (just like you normally can during blockstun)
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge and continues to deplete 50 Drive Gauge per frame (0.3 Drive bars per second) as you hold the Parry
You lose the parry if you go into Burnout from the initial Drive Gauge cost (Perfect Parry requires frame-perfect timing when starting from under 1/2 of a Drive bar)
Rewards 1 bar of Drive Gauge (1/2 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a strike
Rewards 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge (0 bar total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successfully parrying a projectile (with a few exceptions)
Rewards 2 bars of Drive Gauge (1.5 bars total after factoring in the initial parry cost) for successful parrying a Super Art or Drive Impact
Successfully parrying a strike attack splits the regular block pushback between both players (while Perfect Parrying a strike removes all of its pushback)
Successfully parrying a projectile or Drive Impact reduces the pushback of it by half (while Perfect Parrying a projectile or Drive Impact removes all of its pushback)

Your hurtbox and throw box expand slightly towards the opponent for the entire duration of the Parry (Throw box extension starts instantly on frame 1, while the Hurtbox extension starts on frame 3, after the initial 2F Perfect Parry window)
Parrying while crouching maintains a regular crouch hurtbox for the first 2 frames of the parry (during the initial Perfect Parry window)

Dashes forward and enhances the next normal or command normal used out of the Drive Rush with +4 extra hitstun / blockstun, as well as improved juggle properties (Juggle Limit increased by +3 / Juggle Start/Increase reduced to 0)
The +4 bonus frame advantage gained from a Drive Rush enhanced move also stacks with any bonus frame advantage that move may have gained from Counter-Hit, Punish Counter, or Burnout
Grounded special-cancelable normals can also be canceled into Drive Rush on hit or block, as well as from light normals on whiff

Drive Rush maintains buffers (freezes the buffer window) during its start-up and freeze frames - This allows players to do things normally not possible within the normal buffer windows, such as Drive Rushing into a charge special

The initial 10F screen freeze eats the opponent's directional inputs (except Jumps) but does not prevent attacks from being buffered immediately afterward (this can cause inputs to be combined in unintended ways, such as crouch MK + Fireball performing a raw Fireball, or a vertical jump + walk forward turning into a forward jump)
Framedata shown in parentheses refers to the Drive Rush version used from a Parry

Drive Rush recovery is only 24F total (23F if done from a Parry) - but the enhanced dash itself is 46F total (45F if done from Parry) and you can cancel it as early as frame 9 (frame 11 if done from a Parry)

Inputting a direction (walk/crouch/block/jump) after the Drive Rush recovery plays out 2 additional recovery frames before the transition occurs (this allows the user to perform an enhanced crouching or command normal late in the dash by inputting the direction + button simultaneously)

You stop building any form of Drive Gauge on your attacks after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (but you still continue to build Drive gauge as normal if you did Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Applies an extra damage scaling penalty after using a Drive Rush in the MIDDLE of a combo (no damage scaling if you use Drive Rush at the START of a combo)
Costs 3 bars of Drive Gauge if canceled into from a normal attack
Costs 1/2 bar of Drive Gauge if canceled into from Parry (for a total of 1 bar of Drive Gauge when you factor in the initial 1/2 bar cost of the Parry)

Normals used out of Drive Rush carry additional forward momentum (but throws and specials immediately halt all momentum, which can be a useful strategy to punish opponents for trying to interrupt your Drive Rush)
Parry Drive Rush can only be initiated after frame 3 of the Parry (but can be performed immediately after recovering from a successful parry)

Parry Drive Rush cannot be initiated with less than 1/2 of a Drive bar (as the initial Parry cost will put the user into Burnout) - But whiff canceling a Light normal into Drive Rush can achieve a similar effect
Cannot cancel Drive Rush into a Parry (inputting MP+MK results in a MK normal coming out instead)
Drive Rush puts the user into a counter-hit state for the entire duration (this makes it impossible to tech a throw that connects during the dash)
